title: Tier 2 plateau stable content
body: This local fixture hydrates through the brokered Fetch path so repeated daemon requests exercise Tier 2 without relying on an external site. The rendered article is deliberately substantial enough to be recognized as usable page content rather than a decorative shell. It describes a deterministic local workload whose text, network behavior, and response size remain identical across every request. That stability makes the fixture useful for observing resident memory, worker ownership, job retention, and thread counts without introducing remote latency or third-party variability. Each pass loads this JSON document, creates an article in the browser runtime, and returns the same readable prose to the extraction pipeline. The plateau check can therefore prove that JavaScript execution and brokered fetching occurred while keeping the measured workload bounded. Repeating the request should reuse established process resources instead of allowing unbounded metadata, retained payloads, sessions, isolates, or descendants to accumulate. After warmup, ownership counters must return to their baseline and the trailing resident-memory trend must remain within the configured tolerance. This paragraph exists as genuine extraction input, not as padding hidden from readers: it gives the content engine enough coherent language to prefer the hydrated document over the otherwise empty static shell.
